text: Oscar Jaroker. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Jaroker O (2026). glmOJ: Toolkit Count Regression Modeling. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://github.com/ojaroker/glmOJ.
code: @Manual{,   title = {glmOJ: Toolkit for Count Regression Modeling},   author = {Oscar Jaroker},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/ojaroker/glmOJ}, }

title: glmOJ — Count Regression Modeling
text: glmOJ R package provides structured workflow count regression modeling. wraps glm, MASS::glm.nb, pscl::zeroinfl fit, diagnose, interpret count regression models, output reported response scale rather link scale.

title: glmOJ — Count Regression Modeling
text: Count data — non-negative integers like number events, species, visits — require specialized regression models. glmOJ guides analyst four steps: Summarize response variable relationship predictors modeling. Fit one count regression models supported families. Assess model conditions using randomized quantile residuals (RQR) Pearson dispersion ratio. Interpret output response scale (exponentiated coefficients confidence intervals).

title: glmOJ — Count Regression Modeling
text: general-purpose countGLM() fits four families selects best AIC, annotated dispersion zero-inflation diagnostics.

title: glmOJ — Count Regression Modeling
text: summarizeCountData(formula, data) — Produces numerical summaries (mean, variance, variance--mean ratio, zero count) plot count response predictors. plot type adapts number type predictors (histogram, scatter, violin, heatmap, etc.).

title: glmOJ — Count Regression Modeling
text: individual fitter returns exponentiated coefficients 95% Wald confidence intervals, randomized quantile residuals, Pearson dispersion ratio, two-panel diagnostic plot (fitted values vs. RQR | normal Q-Q RQR). poissonGLM(formula, data) — Poisson regression via stats::glm. negbinGLM(formula, data) — Negative binomial regression via MASS::glm.nb. zeroinflPoissonGLM(formula, data, ziformula) — Zero-inflated Poisson via pscl::zeroinfl. Separate coefficient tables count zero-inflation components. zeroinflNegbinGLM(formula, data, ziformula) — Zero-inflated negative binomial via pscl::zeroinfl.

title: glmOJ — Count Regression Modeling
text: countGLM(formula, data, ziformula) — Fits four families, selects best AIC, returns plain-language recommendation informed Pearson dispersion ratio observed vs. expected zero counts.

title: glmOJ — Count Regression Modeling
text: model fitters compute: Randomized quantile residuals (RQR) — well-fitting model, RQRs approximately standard normal. diagnostic plot shows fitted values (checking structure) normal Q-Q plot. Pearson dispersion ratio — Pearson chi-squared divided residual degrees freedom. Values near 1 consistent assumed model; values substantially 1 (rule thumb: > 1.5 Poisson fit) suggest overdispersion motivate switching negative binomial.

title: glmOJ — Count Regression Modeling
text: coefficients reported response scale (exponentiated). count component model, exponentiating coefficient gives multiplicative change expected count one-unit increase predictor, adjusting simultaneous linear changes predictors. example, value 1.5 means 50% higher expected count. zero-inflation component ZI models, exponentiated coefficient gives multiplicative change odds structural zero.

title: glmOJ — Count Regression Modeling

what: Fit and compare count regression models — countGLM
title: Fit and compare count regression models — countGLM
text: Fits four count regression models supported glmOJ (Poisson, negative binomial, zero-inflated Poisson, zero-inflated negative binomial), selects best AIC, provides plain-language recommendation informed dispersion zero-inflation diagnostics.

title: Fit and compare count regression models — countGLM
Fit and compare count regression models — countGLM
text: formula model formula count component (e.g. y ~ x1 + x2). response must non-negative integer count variable. data data frame containing variables formula (ziformula provided). ziformula one-sided formula zero-inflation component passed zeroinflPoissonGLM() zeroinflNegbinGLM(). NULL (default), right-hand side formula used. ... Additional arguments passed individual model fitter.

title: Fit and compare count regression models — countGLM
text: object class "countGLM", list : call matched call. fits named list successfully fitted model objects (poisson, negbin, zeroinfl_poisson, zeroinfl_negbin). model failed converge omitted. aic_table named numeric vector AICs, sorted ascending. best_model Character name model lowest AIC. recommendation plain-language character string explaining selection, including dispersion zero-inflation context.

title: Fit and compare count regression models — countGLM
text: Model selection: AIC primary criterion. Lower AIC indicates better balance fit parsimony. Heuristic diagnostics (computed Poisson fit used annotate recommendation, override AIC): Overdispersion: Pearson dispersion ratio > 1.5 Poisson fit suggests negative binomial family may appropriate. Zero-inflation: observed number zeros exceeds 1.3x number zeros expected Poisson model, excess zeros flagged. Individual models can accessed via result$fits$negbin, etc., support print(), summary(), plot().

title: Fit and compare count regression models — countGLM
code: df <- data.frame(   y  = c(0L, 1L, 2L, 3L, 5L, 0L, 2L, 4L, 1L, 3L),   x1 = c(1.2, -0.4, 0.8, -1.1, 2.0, 0.3, -0.9, 1.5, -0.2, 0.7) ) result <- countGLM(y ~ x1, data = df) #> Warning: iteration limit reached #> Warning: iteration limit reached print(result) #>  #> Call: #> countGLM(formula = y ~ x1, data = df) #>  #> AIC comparison: #>             model   AIC #>           poisson 39.02 #>            negbin 41.02 #>  zeroinfl_poisson 41.43 #>   zeroinfl_negbin 43.43 #>  #> Selected model: poisson #>  #> Recommendation: #>   Poisson was selected by AIC (AIC = 39.02). The Poisson dispersion #>   ratio is 1.17, consistent with equidispersion. There are 1.4x more #>   zeros than expected under Poisson (observed: 2, expected: 1.4), #>   suggesting zero-inflation. #>  summary(result) #> Summary of selected model (poisson): #>  #>  #> Call: #> poissonGLM(formula = formula, data = data) #>  #> Model family: poissonGLM  #>  #> Coefficients (on response scale): #>         term exp.coef lower.95 upper.95 #>  (Intercept)   1.7989   1.0683   3.0290 #>           x1   1.3396   0.8572   2.0936 #>  #> Dispersion ratio: 1.1658 #> AIC: 39.02

what: Fit a negative binomial regression model — negbinGLM
title: Fit a negative binomial regression model — negbinGLM
text: Fits negative binomial GLM (via MASS::glm.nb()) returns model coefficients response scale (exponentiated), randomized quantile residuals (RQR), Pearson dispersion ratio, two-panel diagnostic plot.

title: Fit a negative binomial regression model — negbinGLM
Fit a negative binomial regression model — negbinGLM
text: formula model formula (e.g. y ~ x1 + x2). response must non-negative integer count variable. data data frame containing variables formula. ... Additional arguments passed MASS::glm.nb().

title: Fit a negative binomial regression model — negbinGLM
text: object class c("negbinGLM", "countGLMfit"), list : call matched call. model underlying MASS::glm.nb fit object. theta estimated negative binomial dispersion parameter (smaller values indicate overdispersion). coefficients data frame columns term, exp.coef, lower.95, upper.95 (response/exponentiated scale). diagnostics list : rqr Numeric vector randomized quantile residuals. dispersion_ratio Pearson chi-squared / df.residual. well-fitted negative binomial model near 1. plot patchwork ggplot: fitted values vs RQR (left) normal Q-Q RQR (right). aic AIC fitted model.

title: Fit a negative binomial regression model — negbinGLM
text: Coefficient interpretation: Like Poisson regression, negative binomial regression models log expected count. Exponentiating coefficient gives multiplicative change expected count one-unit increase predictor, adjusting simultaneous linear changes predictors. example, 1.5 means 50% higher expected count. use: Negative binomial appropriate count data show overdispersion (variance > mean). Pearson dispersion ratio poissonGLM() substantially 1 (rule thumb: > 1.5) common signal. negative binomial adds free parameter theta model extra variance.

title: Fit a negative binomial regression model — negbinGLM
code: df <- data.frame(   y  = c(0L, 1L, 2L, 3L, 5L, 0L, 2L, 4L, 1L, 3L),   x1 = c(1.2, -0.4, 0.8, -1.1, 2.0, 0.3, -0.9, 1.5, -0.2, 0.7) ) fit <- negbinGLM(y ~ x1, data = df) #> Warning: iteration limit reached #> Warning: iteration limit reached print(fit) #>  #> Call: #> negbinGLM(formula = y ~ x1, data = df) #>  #> Model family: negbinGLM  #>  #> Coefficients (on response scale): #>         term exp.coef lower.95 upper.95 #>  (Intercept)   1.7989   1.0683   3.0291 #>           x1   1.3396   0.8571   2.0936 #>  #> Dispersion ratio: 1.1657 #> AIC: 41.02 plot(fit)

what: Plot count data against predictors — plot_count_data
title: Plot count data against predictors — plot_count_data
text: Internal plotting function called summarizeCountData. Selects appropriate ggplot based number type predictors supplied.

title: Plot count data against predictors — plot_count_data
Plot count data against predictors — plot_count_data
text: y Numeric vector non-negative integer counts (response). mf model frame returned model.frame. pred_vars Character vector predictor variable names plot. two names supplied, first two used warning issued. pred_types Named character vector classifying element pred_vars either "continuous" "categorical".

title: Plot count data against predictors — plot_count_data
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot count data against predictors — plot_count_data
text: See summarizeCountData full description plot type produced predictor combination.

what: Fit a Poisson regression model — poissonGLM
title: Fit a Poisson regression model — poissonGLM
text: Fits Poisson GLM returns model coefficients response scale (exponentiated), randomized quantile residuals (RQR), Pearson dispersion ratio, two-panel diagnostic plot.

title: Fit a Poisson regression model — poissonGLM
Fit a Poisson regression model — poissonGLM
text: formula model formula (e.g. y ~ x1 + x2). response must non-negative integer count variable. data data frame containing variables formula. ... Additional arguments passed stats::glm().

title: Fit a Poisson regression model — poissonGLM
text: object class c("poissonGLM", "countGLMfit"), list : call matched call. model underlying stats::glm fit object. coefficients data frame columns term, exp.coef, lower.95, upper.95 (response/exponentiated scale). diagnostics list : rqr Numeric vector randomized quantile residuals. dispersion_ratio Pearson chi-squared / df.residual. Values substantially 1 (rule thumb: > 1.5) suggest overdispersion; consider negbinGLM(). plot patchwork ggplot: fitted values vs RQR (left) normal Q-Q RQR (right). aic AIC fitted model.

title: Fit a Poisson regression model — poissonGLM
text: Coefficient interpretation: Poisson regression models log expected count. Exponentiating coefficient gives multiplicative change expected count one-unit increase predictor, adjusting simultaneous linear changes predictors. example, 1.5 means 50% higher expected count. Condition checking: Inspect diagnostics$dispersion_ratio. value near 1 consistent Poisson assumption (mean = variance). RQR diagnostic plot show points scattered randomly around zero approximately normal QQ behaviour.

title: Fit a Poisson regression model — poissonGLM
code: df <- data.frame(   y  = c(0L, 1L, 2L, 3L, 5L, 0L, 2L, 4L, 1L, 3L),   x1 = c(1.2, -0.4, 0.8, -1.1, 2.0, 0.3, -0.9, 1.5, -0.2, 0.7) ) fit <- poissonGLM(y ~ x1, data = df) print(fit) #>  #> Call: #> poissonGLM(formula = y ~ x1, data = df) #>  #> Model family: poissonGLM  #>  #> Coefficients (on response scale): #>         term exp.coef lower.95 upper.95 #>  (Intercept)   1.7989   1.0683   3.0290 #>           x1   1.3396   0.8572   2.0936 #>  #> Dispersion ratio: 1.1658 #> AIC: 39.02 plot(fit)

title: Summarize count data — summarizeCountData
text: Produces numerical graphical summary count response variable, plot automatically adapting number type predictors formula.

title: Summarize count data — summarizeCountData
Summarize count data — summarizeCountData
text: formula formula form y ~ x1 + x2 + ... y non-negative integer count response. Offsets via offset() supported. data data frame containing variables formula.

title: Summarize count data — summarizeCountData
text: named list three elements: summary one-row data.frame containing: mean, var, var_mean_ratio, n_zero, n_total. counts data.frame columns count freq giving frequency observed count value. plot ggplot object. plot type depends number type predictors — see Details.

title: Summarize count data — summarizeCountData
text: graphical summary chosen based predictors formula: predictors Histogram count response. One continuous predictor Scatter plot loess smooth. One categorical predictor Violin plot jittered points. Two continuous predictors 2D bin plot (geom_bin2d) viridis fill scale. Two categorical predictors Tile heatmap mean counts. One continuous, one categorical predictor Scatter plot loess smooths coloured categorical variable. Three predictors warning issued first two predictors used. var_mean_ratio summary table variance--mean ratio. value close 1 consistent Poisson distribution; values substantially greater 1 suggest overdispersion.

title: Summarize count data — summarizeCountData
code: set.seed(1) df <- data.frame(   y  = rpois(100, lambda = 3),   x1 = rnorm(100),   x2 = sample(c("A", "B"), 100, replace = TRUE) )  # No predictors summarizeCountData(y ~ 1, data = df) #> $summary #>   mean     var var_mean_ratio n_zero n_total #> 1 3.05 2.14899      0.7045869      2     100 #>  #> $counts #>   count freq #> 1     0    2 #> 2     1   11 #> 3     2   28 #> 4     3   20 #> 5     4   23 #> 6     5   12 #> 7     6    3 #> 8     8    1 #>  #> $plot  #>   # One continuous predictor summarizeCountData(y ~ x1, data = df) #> $summary #>   mean     var var_mean_ratio n_zero n_total #> 1 3.05 2.14899      0.7045869      2     100 #>  #> $counts #>   count freq #> 1     0    2 #> 2     1   11 #> 3     2   28 #> 4     3   20 #> 5     4   23 #> 6     5   12 #> 7     6    3 #> 8     8    1 #>  #> $plot #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'  #>   # One categorical predictor summarizeCountData(y ~ x2, data = df) #> $summary #>   mean     var var_mean_ratio n_zero n_total #> 1 3.05 2.14899      0.7045869      2     100 #>  #> $counts #>   count freq #> 1     0    2 #> 2     1   11 #> 3     2   28 #> 4     3   20 #> 5     4   23 #> 6     5   12 #> 7     6    3 #> 8     8    1 #>  #> $plot  #>   # Mixed predictors summarizeCountData(y ~ x1 + x2, data = df) #> $summary #>   mean     var var_mean_ratio n_zero n_total #> 1 3.05 2.14899      0.7045869      2     100 #>  #> $counts #>   count freq #> 1     0    2 #> 2     1   11 #> 3     2   28 #> 4     3   20 #> 5     4   23 #> 6     5   12 #> 7     6    3 #> 8     8    1 #>  #> $plot #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'  #>

what: Fit a zero-inflated negative binomial regression model — zeroinflNegbinGLM
title: Fit a zero-inflated negative binomial regression model — zeroinflNegbinGLM
text: Fits zero-inflated negative binomial model (via pscl::zeroinfl()) separate count zero-inflation components. Returns coefficients response scale, randomized quantile residuals, dispersion ratio, diagnostic plot.

title: Fit a zero-inflated negative binomial regression model — zeroinflNegbinGLM
Fit a zero-inflated negative binomial regression model — zeroinflNegbinGLM
text: formula model formula count component (e.g. y ~ x1 + x2). response must non-negative integer count. data data frame containing variables formula (ziformula provided). ziformula one-sided formula zero-inflation component (e.g. ~ x1). NULL (default), right-hand side formula used components. Use ~ 1 intercept-zero-inflation model. ... Additional arguments passed pscl::zeroinfl().

title: Fit a zero-inflated negative binomial regression model — zeroinflNegbinGLM
text: object class c("zeroinflNegbinGLM", "zeroinflGLMfit", "countGLMfit"), list : call matched call. model underlying pscl::zeroinfl fit object. theta estimated negative binomial dispersion parameter. coefficients list two data frames, columns term, exp.coef, lower.95, upper.95: count Exponentiated coefficients count component. zero Exponentiated coefficients zero-inflation component. diagnostics list rqr, dispersion_ratio, plot (see poissonGLM() details). aic AIC fitted model.

title: Fit a zero-inflated negative binomial regression model — zeroinflNegbinGLM
text: Coefficient interpretation: Count component: exponentiating coefficient gives multiplicative change expected count among non-structural-zero observations, one-unit increase predictor, adjusting simultaneous linear changes predictors. example, 1.5 means 50% higher expected count. Zero component: exponentiating coefficient gives multiplicative change odds structural zero (vs. entering count process) one-unit increase predictor. use: Zero-inflated negative binomial flexible four models — handles excess zeros overdispersion non-zero counts. Prefer zeroinflPoissonGLM() non-zero counts remain overdispersed zero-inflation accounted .

title: Fit a zero-inflated negative binomial regression model — zeroinflNegbinGLM
code: df <- data.frame(   y  = c(0L, 0L, 0L, 1L, 2L, 0L, 3L, 0L, 1L, 0L),   x1 = c(1.2, -0.4, 0.8, -1.1, 2.0, 0.3, -0.9, 1.5, -0.2, 0.7) ) fit <- zeroinflNegbinGLM(y ~ x1, data = df) print(fit) #>  #> Call: #> zeroinflNegbinGLM(formula = y ~ x1, data = df) #>  #> Model family: zeroinflNegbinGLM  #>  #> Count component (exponentiated coefficients): #>         term exp.coef lower.95 upper.95 #>  (Intercept)   1.3408   0.4922   3.6523 #>           x1   0.9433   0.4383   2.0304 #>  #> Zero-inflation component (exponentiated coefficients): #>         term exp.coef lower.95 upper.95 #>  (Intercept)   0.6737   0.0820   5.5325 #>           x1   2.1030   0.4077  10.8486 #>  #> Dispersion ratio: 2.0053 #> AIC: 31.60 plot(fit)   # Intercept-only zero component: fit2 <- zeroinflNegbinGLM(y ~ x1, data = df, ziformula = ~ 1)

what: Fit a zero-inflated Poisson regression model — zeroinflPoissonGLM
title: Fit a zero-inflated Poisson regression model — zeroinflPoissonGLM
text: Fits zero-inflated Poisson model (via pscl::zeroinfl()) separate count zero-inflation components. Returns coefficients response scale, randomized quantile residuals, dispersion ratio, diagnostic plot.

title: Fit a zero-inflated Poisson regression model — zeroinflPoissonGLM
Fit a zero-inflated Poisson regression model — zeroinflPoissonGLM
text: formula model formula count component (e.g. y ~ x1 + x2). response must non-negative integer count. data data frame containing variables formula (ziformula provided). ziformula one-sided formula zero-inflation component (e.g. ~ x1). NULL (default), right-hand side formula used components. Use ~ 1 intercept-zero-inflation model. ... Additional arguments passed pscl::zeroinfl().

title: Fit a zero-inflated Poisson regression model — zeroinflPoissonGLM
text: object class c("zeroinflPoissonGLM", "zeroinflGLMfit", "countGLMfit"), list : call matched call. model underlying pscl::zeroinfl fit object. coefficients list two data frames, columns term, exp.coef, lower.95, upper.95: count Exponentiated coefficients count component. zero Exponentiated coefficients zero-inflation component. diagnostics list rqr, dispersion_ratio, plot (see poissonGLM() details). aic AIC fitted model.

title: Fit a zero-inflated Poisson regression model — zeroinflPoissonGLM
text: Coefficient interpretation: Count component: exponentiating coefficient gives multiplicative change expected count among non-structural-zero observations, one-unit increase predictor, adjusting simultaneous linear changes predictors. example, 1.5 means 50% higher expected count. Zero component: exponentiating coefficient gives multiplicative change odds structural zero (vs. entering count process) one-unit increase predictor. value greater 1 means higher odds structural zero. use: Zero-inflated Poisson appropriate zeros standard Poisson model predict (excess zeros), non-zero counts follow Poisson distribution. non-zero counts also overdispersed, prefer zeroinflNegbinGLM().

title: Fit a zero-inflated Poisson regression model — zeroinflPoissonGLM
code: df <- data.frame(   y  = c(0L, 0L, 0L, 1L, 2L, 0L, 3L, 0L, 1L, 0L),   x1 = c(1.2, -0.4, 0.8, -1.1, 2.0, 0.3, -0.9, 1.5, -0.2, 0.7) ) fit <- zeroinflPoissonGLM(y ~ x1, data = df) print(fit) #>  #> Call: #> zeroinflPoissonGLM(formula = y ~ x1, data = df) #>  #> Model family: zeroinflPoissonGLM  #>  #> Count component (exponentiated coefficients): #>         term exp.coef lower.95 upper.95 #>  (Intercept)   1.3408   0.4923   3.6521 #>           x1   0.9433   0.4383   2.0303 #>  #> Zero-inflation component (exponentiated coefficients): #>         term exp.coef lower.95 upper.95 #>  (Intercept)   0.6737   0.0821   5.5318 #>           x1   2.1030   0.4077  10.8484 #>  #> Dispersion ratio: 1.6711 #> AIC: 29.60 plot(fit)   # Intercept-only zero component: fit2 <- zeroinflPoissonGLM(y ~ x1, data = df, ziformula = ~ 1)
